<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Biome reordered imports. `getPersonaId` now appears before `withPersonaCookie`. The real `next/headers` is used instead of the mock.

A side-effect-only import of the helper file at the top of the test file will ensure the mock loads before the module under test.

Separating imports by group with blank lines might not prevent Biome from reordering them.

Duplicating the mock inside the test file would repeat the helper logic. A side-effect import with a biome ignore comment is the cleaner fix.

Biome may still reorder imports even with a side-effect import. The root cause is that Vitest does not hoist `vi.mock` from a dependency file when that dependency loads after the mocked module. The mock must therefore be registered before the module under test is imported.</details></think>

**S3 complete** — persona fixtures, cookie mock-auth, and DB seeding are in place with **T13–T14** passing (52 tests total).

## Scope delivered

| Test | What it verifies |
|---|---|
| **T13** | 7 personas; each starting I&E parses zod; `riley` excluded from seed (6 personas) |
| **T14** | `getPersonaId()` returns `'pat'` / `null` via mocked `next/headers` |

## Files added/changed

- **`lib/identity/persona-cookie.ts`** — `getPersonaId()` and `setPersonaId()` (HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, 30-day max-age)
- **`lib/personas.ts`** — `getPersonasForSeeding()` (all except `riley`)
- **`lib/db/client.ts`** — on first DB open, if `snapshots` is empty, seeds starting snapshots for 6 personas via `assess()`
- **`tests/s3/t13-persona-fixtures.test.ts`**, **`tests/s3/t14-persona-cookie.test.ts`**
- **`vitest-setup.ts`** — loads the `withPersonaCookie` mock globally (Biome import sorting was loading real `next/headers` before the mock)

Persona fixture definitions in `lib/personas.ts` were already shipped in S1 for T8; this slice adds auth + seeding.

## Checks

- `npm test` — 52 passed
- `npm run lint` — pass
- `npm run typecheck` — pass

## Out of scope (per test plan)

Middleware redirect for unauthenticated `/dashboard*` / `/history` visits is manual integration, not covered by T13–T14.

## Next slice

Per tech-spec build order: **`/implement S9`** (framing copy + `<FramingNotice />`, T43 + T32) before S4–S6.

Session snapshot: `docs/ai/sessions/S012-implement-s3.md`. Changes are uncommitted — say if you want a commit or PR.

diff --git a/lib/db/client.ts b/lib/db/client.ts
index 078299c..f57a7a5 100644
--- a/lib/db/client.ts
+++ b/lib/db/client.ts
@@ -1,12 +1,39 @@
 import Database from "better-sqlite3";
+import { count } from "drizzle-orm";
+import type { BetterSQLite3Database } from "drizzle-orm/better-sqlite3";
 import { drizzle } from "drizzle-orm/better-sqlite3";
+import { assess } from "../affordability/calculator";
+import { getPersonasForSeeding } from "../personas";
 import { applyMigrations } from "./migrate";
+import { snapshots } from "./schema";
 import { createSnapshotRepository, type SnapshotRepository } from "./snapshots";
 
 const DB_PATH = &#96;${process.cwd()}/.data/financial-health.sqlite&#96;;
 
 let repository: SnapshotRepository | null = null;
 
+function seedStartingSnapshotsIfEmpty(
+  db: BetterSQLite3Database,
+  snapshotRepository: SnapshotRepository,
+): void {
+  const [{ value: rowCount }] = db
+    .select({ value: count() })
+    .from(snapshots)
+    .all();
+  if (rowCount &gt; 0) {
+    return;
+  }
+
+  for (const persona of getPersonasForSeeding()) {
+    const outcome = assess(persona.startingIe);
+    snapshotRepository.createSnapshot({
+      customerId: persona.id,
+      ie: persona.startingIe,
+      outcome,
+    });
+  }
+}
+
 export function getSnapshotRepository(): SnapshotRepository {
   if (repository) {
     return repository;
@@ -16,6 +43,7 @@ export function getSnapshotRepository(): SnapshotRepository {
   const db = drizzle(sqlite);
   applyMigrations(db, DB_PATH);
   repository = createSnapshotRepository(db);
+  seedStartingSnapshotsIfEmpty(db, repository);
   return repository;
 }
 
diff --git a/lib/personas.ts b/lib/personas.ts
index e52ae12..5d3e3a1 100644
--- a/lib/personas.ts
+++ b/lib/personas.ts
@@ -119,3 +119,8 @@ export const personas: Persona[] = [
 export function getPersonaById(id: string): Persona | undefined {
   return personas.find((persona) =&gt; persona.id === id);
 }
+
+/** Personas that receive a starting snapshot when the DB is first opened. */
+export function getPersonasForSeeding(): Persona[] {
+  return personas.filter((persona) =&gt; persona.id !== "riley");
+}
diff --git a/vitest-setup.ts b/vitest-setup.ts

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Gathering context for the critic review.

Reviewing the critic implementation against the tech spec reveals several gaps. Middleware is missing. setPersonaId is untested. Seeding lacks integration tests. T8 touches S3, though the persona matrix is already in S1. The signature mismatch between sync and async getPersonaId is likely acceptable for Next.js 15+. Seeding timing differs slightly from the spec. R10 seeding logs are covered by T12.

getPersonaId accepts arbitrary strings without validating against known personas. The vitest setup applies a global mock that affects all tests. Riley filtering relies on a hardcoded ID rather than the expected outcome field, so adding another no-data persona would break skipping. Empty table seeds correctly stay empty if all snapshots are deleted manually. Whether middleware belongs in the current S3 scope or should be deferred still needs assessment.

The missing middleware is an acceptable deferral. The test plan explicitly defers the cookie redirect to manual integration, so it is not a gate failure.

T14 only covers getPersonaId, so setPersonaId remains untested. T13 validates the seeding helper but not the full client.ts integration, yet the tech spec does not require that seed integration test. Seed validation is skipped because personas are trusted fixtures.

The cookie stores only a persona identifier with no personal data. It omits an explicit path attribute and may lack a Secure flag for local development. The seed path logs are suppressed because createSnapshot does not emit per-request logs under S2.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
A conflict exists between documentation and the S3 tech spec. NOTES.md states fixtures live in the repository. The S3 spec instead mandates database seeding on first open.

The fixture storage conflict is resolved upstream, not a bug. Riley's empty startingIe arrays pass Zod validation. The critic verdict is minor fixes. Middleware is missing from T13/T14 despite appearing in the S3 design. Session documents correctly. The test plan defers middleware to manual integration.

The middleware gap is minor rather than rework because the test plan defers it manually. Seeding lacks integration tests; T13 validates a helper instead of the actual repository seed path. This weakens R4 for the seed path. setPersonaId is implemented per the tech spec but has no test coverage.

The mock cookie store lacks a `set` method. S5 avoids this by pre-setting state with `withPersonaCookie`. `setPersonaId` would fail in tests if invoked. The persona selector page is not yet built.

The tech spec lists `getPersonaId` as synchronous, but the implementation correctly returns a Promise for Next.js 15. This is a minor documentation drift.

`getPersonasForSeeding` hardcodes the riley ID. Filtering by expected outcome would be more maintainable.

A global vitest mock was added intentionally to align with S7. It risks masking underlying issues, though the change is test-only.

T13 does not assert the exact £-values pinned in S3. It only verifies Zod parsing. The traceability to pinned values is through code review rather than test assertions.

Persona IDs are not validated. getPersonaId and setPersonaId accept any string. This is acceptable for the take-home scope.

Seeding calls createSnapshot without logging. This is acceptable per S2.

The session snapshot claims "None" for deviations while simultaneously documenting the unimplemented middleware redirect. This contradiction is a minor documentation issue.

No serious gate failures were found. The implementation stays within the S3 scope, and tests match T13 and T14. The PRD, tech spec, and test plan were not modified inappropriately.

Cookie design follows data minimisation principles. The cookie is HttpOnly, contains no PII, and omits the Secure flag. Mock authentication is disclosed in discovery A-2.

The top two fixes are recording the middleware omission as a known deferral, and extending the `withPersonaCookie` mock with a `set` stub for future persona selector tests.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

## Critic review — Phase 5: `/implement S3`

**Artefact:** S3 implementation diff (uncommitted on `main`, start commit `f929b44`)  
**Upstream inputs:** `docs/TECH_SPEC.md` §S3, `docs/TEST_PLAN.md` T8/T13/T14, `docs/PRD.md` R8/R4, `docs/discovery/NOTES.md` A-2

---

### Verdict

**Minor fixes** — slice is commit-worthy for its assigned test scope (T13–T14 green, 52 tests total). A few spec-vs-implementation gaps are acceptable deferrals per the test plan, but they should be recorded explicitly rather than under “No deviations.”

---

### Missing or unsupported claims

- **Middleware redirect is in TECH_SPEC S3 but not shipped.** Spec says unauthenticated `/dashboard*` / `/history` visitors are redirected to `/`. TEST_PLAN §1 defers “cookie redirect” to manual integration. The session snapshot says “Deviations: None” while also noting middleware is manual — those contradict.
- **Seeding behaviour is not integration-tested.** `lib/db/client.ts` seeds on first repository open, but T13 only asserts `getPersonasForSeeding()` — not that six snapshots are actually written. TEST_PLAN §1 also defers “seed-on-first-request” to manual walkthrough, so this is a known automation gap, not an silent failure.
- **`setPersonaId` is implemented but untested.** TECH_SPEC S3 requires it; T14 covers only `getPersonaId`. Acceptable today because no `T*` requires `set`, but the helper mock also lacks `cookies().set`, so future persona-selector work (likely S8) will need mock extension.
- **Pinned £-values (TECH_SPEC traceability for R8) are not asserted in T13.** Values live in `lib/personas.ts` (shipped S1); T8 covers outcome states. T13 only checks zod parse + count. Traceability to “pinned values” is by code inspection, not test assertion.
- **Async API vs spec pseudocode.** TECH_SPEC shows sync `getPersonaId(): string | null`; implementation correctly uses `async`/`Promise` for Next.js `cookies()`. Harmless spec drift, not an implementation bug.

---

### Gate failures

- **None that block the phase gate.** Tests land with implementation; scope stays within S3; no upstream artefact edits to paper over gaps.
- **Latent spec/test-plan tension (pre-existing):** TECH_SPEC S3 prose includes middleware; TEST_PLAN assigns it to manual integration with no `T*`. Implementation correctly followed the test plan, but the tech spec reads as if middleware is in-slice.

---

### Traceability gaps

| Item | Gap |
|---|---|
| **R8** | Covered via T13 + prior T8 (personas + outcomes). T13 does not re-assert per-persona pence from the S3 table. |
| **R4** | T13 satisfies fixture-shape protection; seed execution path relies on manual integration per test plan. |
| **T8 → S3** | T8 still marked Pending in TEST_PLAN §7 status table (stale tracking, not a code gap). |
| **A-2 (mock auth)** | Implemented; no automated test that arbitrary cookie values are rejected (not required by any `T*`). |

---

### Conduct, regulatory, accessibility blind spots

- **Data minimisation (R10):** Cookie stores only a synthetic persona id; HttpOnly + SameSite=Lax match spec. Seeding reuses `createSnapshot` (no extra logging beyond S2 hygiene). No concern.
- **Mock auth (A-2):** No validation that the cookie value is one of the seven personas — acceptable for take-home mock auth, but S4/S5 should handle unknown ids gracefully (out of S3 scope).
- **Cookie `Secure` flag:** Not set — fine for local dev; production would need HTTPS context (likely S8/README territory).
- **Vulnerable users:** S3 is infrastructure only; no customer-facing copy yet. No a11y surface in this slice.

---

### Top three things to fix first

1. **Fix the session snapshot contradiction** — either list middleware as a documented deferral (not “No deviations”), or implement `middleware.ts` in a later slice with an explicit handoff note. Do not leave “None” and a middleware caveat in the same section.
2. **Extend `withPersonaCookie` mock with `cookies().set`** before S8 persona-selector or any test that calls `setPersonaId`. Stub can mirror the `get` behaviour using `PERSONA_COOKIE_NAME` from `lib/identity/persona-cookie.ts` to avoid string drift.
3. **Decide seeding test coverage explicitly** — either add a small integration test (`getSnapshotRepository()` on temp DB → 6 rows, no `riley`) or keep manual-only and note it in S012 + README handoff for S8 (R14 reviewer walkthrough).

---

### Summary

The core S3 deliverables are in place: persona fixtures (pre-existing), `getPersonasForSeeding()`, cookie helpers, seed-on-empty-DB, and T13–T14. The main gaps are **middleware** (spec prose vs test-plan deferral), **untested seed execution**, and **untested/unmocked `setPersonaId`**. None block commit for this slice if deferrals are recorded honestly; address items 1–2 before S4/S8 to avoid surprise failures downstream.

---

_**User**_

fix those top three things

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Fixing the top three issues from the critic review.

The session snapshot contradiction needs fixing by either documenting middleware as a deferral or implementing `middleware.ts` with an explicit handoff note. The `withPersonaCookie` mock must include `cookies().set` before persona-selector tests that call `setPersonaId`, using `PERSONA_COOKIE_NAME` from `lib/identity/persona-cookie.ts`. Seeding test coverage should be decided explicitly—either add a small integration test or keep manual-only and document it in S012 and the README handoff for S8.

S012 will be updated to document middleware as a deferral rather than "No deviations". The `withPersonaCookie` mock will be extended with `cookies().set` using the `PERSONA_COOKIE_NAME` constant. For the seeding integration test, I am weighing two approaches: exporting `seedStartingSnapshotsIfEmpty` for a temporary database setup, or using a temporary DB path. The current `client.ts` relies on a hardcoded database path and a singleton repository, which complicates test isolation.

Directly testing `getSnapshotRepository()` would hit the production `.data` path and singleton repository, so that approach is rejected. The best path forward is to duplicate the seeding logic inside a test using `makeDb()` and an inline seed call, rather than extracting a separate module or altering the client configuration.

The cleanest minimal approach is to export `seedStartingSnapshotsIfEmpty` from `client.ts` or a new `lib/db/seed.ts` module. Tests can then use `makeDb()` to create an empty in-memory database, call the exported seed function directly, and assert six snapshots for six persona IDs while confirming Riley has none. This avoids duplicating seeding logic inside tests.

The seeding function will live in a new `lib/db/seed.ts` module to keep `client.ts` clean. For the session snapshot contradiction, the user may want the middleware file implemented rather than just documented.

The session snapshot contradiction will be fixed by documenting middleware as a deferral rather than implementing `middleware.ts` now. The `withPersonaCookie` mock extension with `cookies().set` will likely be added to T14 or a small dedicated test. An integration test for seeding will be added to cover the snapshot repository behavior.

Confirmed no circular dependency exists between the test-only `withPersonaCookie` helper and the library cookie constants. The `cookies().set` mock will be wired through `vi.mock` on `next/headers`, using `PERSONA_COOKIE_NAME` to conditionally return the active persona ID.

The `vi.mock` factory cannot access imported constants directly because Vitest hoists mocks. The `PERSONA_COOKIE_NAME` constant must be declared with `vi.hoisted` inside the factory, following the pattern used in S009, so the mock can correctly handle `cookies().set`.

The `withPersonaCookie` mock will import `PERSONA_COOKIE_NAME` directly at the top level instead of using `vi.hoisted`. The `cookies().set` mock will check the cookie name against this imported constant.

Vitest hoists `vi.mock` before imports, so the `withPersonaCookie` mock will use `vi.hoisted` to declare `personaState` with `activePersonaId` and hardcode the cookie name `"personaId"` inside the factory. This avoids importing the `PERSONA_COOKIE_NAME` constant directly.

Reconsidering the `withPersonaCookie` mock strategy. Since Vitest 4 runs `vi.mock` factories lazily after imports resolve, the mock factory may actually be able to import `PERSONA_COOKIE_NAME` directly rather than hardcoding the cookie name or using `vi.hoisted`.

T14 confirmed the global `vitest-setup` import of `withPersonaCookie` works. The helper will use `vi.hoisted` for shared state and add a `set` method. `PERSONA_COOKIE_NAME` will be imported directly in the test helper file, since Vitest hoists `vi.mock` but allows referencing imported bindings in the factory.

The `withPersonaCookie` helper will expose `getActivePersonaIdForTests` to read the active persona ID during tests. A T14 assertion will verify that `PERSONA_COOKIE_NAME` matches the hardcoded cookie name. T14 will also test that `setPersonaId` installs the cookie so `getPersonaId` resolves to the same value.</details></think>
